schema.name: PositionReport

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this Class A position report it is 1 for a scheduled report, 2 for an assigned-schedule report or 3 for a special / interrogation-response report (the three variants share an identical field layout).

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

NavigationalStatus.description: Navigational status code (ITU-R M.1371): 0 = under way using engine, 1 = at anchor, 2 = not under command, 3 = restricted manoeuvrability, 4 = constrained by her draught, 5 = moored, 6 = aground, 7 = engaged in fishing, 8 = under way sailing, 9-13 = reserved/special categories, 14 = AIS-SART/MOB/EPIRB active, 15 = undefined (default).

RateOfTurn.description: Rate of turn as the AIS-encoded value in the range -128..127. 0 = not turning; positive = turning to starboard (right), negative = turning to port (left); +127/-127 = turning faster than 5 degrees per 30 s; -128 = no turn information available. Decoded degrees per minute = (value/4.733) squared, carrying the sign of the value.

Sog.description: Speed over ground in knots (aisstream.io reports the decoded value; AIS native resolution is 0.1 kn, 102.3 = not available).

PositionAccuracy.description: Position accuracy flag: true = high accuracy (better than 10 m, e.g. DGNSS-corrected), false = low accuracy (greater than 10 m, autonomous GNSS).

Longitude.description: Longitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, east positive (range -180 to 180; 181 = position not available).

Latitude.description: Latitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, north positive (range -90 to 90; 91 = position not available).

Cog.description: Course over ground in degrees true (0-359.9; the encoded value 3600, i.e. 360 degrees, means not available).

TrueHeading.description: Heading in degrees true from the vessel's compass or gyro (0-359); 511 = heading not available.

Timestamp.description: UTC second of the minute (0-59) at which the report was generated by the originating station's electronic position-fixing system; 60 = time stamp not available (default), 61 = positioning system in manual input mode, 62 = positioning system in dead-reckoning mode, 63 = positioning system inoperative.

SpecialManoeuvreIndicator.description: Special manoeuvre indicator: 0 = not available (default), 1 = not engaged in special manoeuvre, 2 = engaged in special manoeuvre (e.g. a regional passing arrangement).

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Raim.description: Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring (RAIM) flag of the position-fixing device: true = RAIM in use, false = RAIM not in use.

CommunicationState.description: SOTDMA/ITDMA communication state value carrying the synchronisation state and slot time-out / slot-allocation information used by the AIS TDMA link layer.

schema.description: Class A Automatic Identification System (AIS) position report (ITU-R M.1371 messages 1, 2 and 3) as decoded and relayed by the aisstream.io firehose. It reports a Class A vessel's instantaneous navigational situation - position, course, speed, heading, rate of turn and navigational status - together with the TDMA communication state used for slot management.

schema.name: ShipStaticData

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this static and voyage-related data report it is 5.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

AisVersion.description: AIS protocol version the station complies with: 0 = ITU-R M.1371-1; values 1, 2 and 3 indicate later editions of the standard.

ImoNumber.description: IMO ship identification number (a seven-digit number that stays with the hull for its lifetime); 0 = not available or not applicable.

CallSign.description: International radio call sign of the vessel as up to 7 six-bit ASCII characters (right-padded with '@'); an all-'@' value means not available.

Name.description: Name of the vessel as up to 20 six-bit ASCII characters (right-padded with '@'); an all-'@' value means not available.

Type.description: Ship and cargo type code (0-99): the tens digit gives the category (3x = special craft, 6x = passenger, 7x = cargo, 8x = tanker, 9x = other) and the units digit the subtype or hazard class. 0 = not available.

Dimension.name: Dimension

A.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the bow of the vessel, in metres (overall length = A + B). 0 = not available.

B.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the stern of the vessel, in metres. 0 = not available.

C.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the port side of the vessel, in metres (beam = C + D). 0 = not available.

D.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the starboard side of the vessel, in metres. 0 = not available.

Dimension.description: Reference point for the reported position and overall vessel dimensions, given as distances in metres from the position-fixing antenna to the bow (A), stern (B), port side (C) and starboard side (D); overall length = A + B and beam = C + D.

FixType.description: Type of electronic position-fixing device: 0 = undefined, 1 = GPS, 2 = GLONASS, 3 = combined GPS/GLONASS, 4 = Loran-C, 5 = Chayka, 6 = integrated navigation system, 7 = surveyed, 8 = Galileo, 15 = internal GNSS.

Eta.name: Eta

Month.description: Estimated time of arrival - month (UTC), 1-12; 0 = not available.

Day.description: Estimated time of arrival - day of month (UTC), 1-31; 0 = not available.

Hour.description: Estimated time of arrival - hour (UTC), 0-23; 24 = not available.

Minute.description: Estimated time of arrival - minute (UTC), 0-59; 60 = not available.

Eta.description: Estimated time of arrival (ETA) at destination, in UTC, encoded as separate month, day, hour and minute fields.

MaximumStaticDraught.description: Maximum present static draught of the vessel in metres (AIS resolution 0.1 m); 0 = not available, 25.5 = 25.5 m or greater.

Destination.description: Free-text destination of the current voyage as up to 20 six-bit ASCII characters (right-padded with '@'), typically a UN/LOCODE or port name; an all-'@' value means not available.

Dte.description: Data terminal equipment (DTE) readiness flag: false = DTE ready/available, true = DTE not available.

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

schema.description: Class A static and voyage-related data report (ITU-R M.1371 message 5) relayed by aisstream.io. It carries the transmitting vessel's identity (MMSI, IMO number, call sign and name), ship and cargo type, overall dimensions and antenna reference point, electronic fix type, maximum static draught, destination and estimated time of arrival.

schema.name: StandardClassBPositionReport

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this standard Class B position report it is 18.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Spare1.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Sog.description: Speed over ground in knots (aisstream.io reports the decoded value; AIS native resolution is 0.1 kn, 102.3 = not available).

PositionAccuracy.description: Position accuracy flag: true = high accuracy (better than 10 m, e.g. DGNSS-corrected), false = low accuracy (greater than 10 m, autonomous GNSS).

Longitude.description: Longitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, east positive (range -180 to 180; 181 = position not available).

Latitude.description: Latitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, north positive (range -90 to 90; 91 = position not available).

Cog.description: Course over ground in degrees true (0-359.9; the encoded value 3600, i.e. 360 degrees, means not available).

Timestamp.description: UTC second of the minute (0-59) at which the report was generated by the originating station's electronic position-fixing system; 60 = time stamp not available (default), 61 = positioning system in manual input mode, 62 = positioning system in dead-reckoning mode, 63 = positioning system inoperative.

Spare2.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

ClassBUnit.description: Class B unit type flag: true = 'CS' (carrier-sense) unit, false = 'SO' (self-organising) unit.

ClassBDisplay.description: Class B capability flag: true = the unit is equipped with a display showing received AIS messages.

ClassBDsc.description: Class B capability flag: true = the unit is equipped with a DSC (digital selective calling) function.

ClassBBand.description: Class B capability flag: true = the unit can operate over the whole marine band (both AIS channels), false = limited band.

ClassBMsg22.description: Class B capability flag: true = the unit can accept channel-management commands via Message 22, false = not capable.

AssignedMode.description: Assigned-mode flag: true = the station's reporting behaviour is controlled by a base station (assigned mode, via message 16/23), false = autonomous mode.

Raim.description: Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring (RAIM) flag of the position-fixing device: true = RAIM in use, false = RAIM not in use.

CommunicationStateIsItdma.description: Indicates how to interpret CommunicationState: true = the station is using ITDMA (incremental) access, false = SOTDMA (self-organising) access.

CommunicationState.description: SOTDMA/ITDMA communication state value carrying the synchronisation state and slot time-out / slot-allocation information used by the AIS TDMA link layer.

schema.description: Standard Class B equipment position report (ITU-R M.1371 message 18) relayed by aisstream.io. Class B 'CS' and 'SO' shipborne mobile equipment use it to report position, speed and course over ground; it also advertises the unit's display, DSC, band and Message 22 capabilities.

Name.description: Name of the vessel as up to 20 six-bit ASCII characters (right-padded with '@'); an all-'@' value means not available.

Type.description: Ship and cargo type code (0-99): the tens digit gives the category (3x = special craft, 6x = passenger, 7x = cargo, 8x = tanker, 9x = other) and the units digit the subtype or hazard class. 0 = not available.

Dimension.name: Dimension

A.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the bow of the vessel, in metres (overall length = A + B). 0 = not available.

B.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the stern of the vessel, in metres. 0 = not available.

C.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the port side of the vessel, in metres (beam = C + D). 0 = not available.

D.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the starboard side of the vessel, in metres. 0 = not available.

Dimension.description: Reference point for the reported position and overall vessel dimensions, given as distances in metres from the position-fixing antenna to the bow (A), stern (B), port side (C) and starboard side (D); overall length = A + B and beam = C + D.

FixType.description: Type of electronic position-fixing device: 0 = undefined, 1 = GPS, 2 = GLONASS, 3 = combined GPS/GLONASS, 4 = Loran-C, 5 = Chayka, 6 = integrated navigation system, 7 = surveyed, 8 = Galileo, 15 = internal GNSS.

Raim.description: Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring (RAIM) flag of the position-fixing device: true = RAIM in use, false = RAIM not in use.

Dte.description: Data terminal equipment (DTE) readiness flag: false = DTE ready/available, true = DTE not available.

AssignedMode.description: Assigned-mode flag: true = the station's reporting behaviour is controlled by a base station (assigned mode, via message 16/23), false = autonomous mode.

Spare3.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

schema.description: Extended Class B equipment position report (ITU-R M.1371 message 19) relayed by aisstream.io. In addition to position, course and speed it carries the vessel name, ship and cargo type, overall dimensions and antenna reference point, and electronic fix type.

schema.name: AidsToNavigationReport

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this aids-to-navigation report it is 21.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Type.description: Aid-to-Navigation type code (0-31) per ITU-R M.1371 (e.g. 1 = reference point, 5 = light without sectors, 9 = beacon, 20-31 = cardinal, lateral, special and safe-water marks); 0 = default/type not specified.

Name.description: Name of the aid to navigation as up to 20 six-bit ASCII characters (right-padded with '@'); continued in NameExtension when longer than 20 characters.

PositionAccuracy.description: Position accuracy flag: true = high accuracy (better than 10 m, e.g. DGNSS-corrected), false = low accuracy (greater than 10 m, autonomous GNSS).

Longitude.description: Longitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, east positive (range -180 to 180; 181 = position not available).

Latitude.description: Latitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, north positive (range -90 to 90; 91 = position not available).

Dimension.name: Dimension

A.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the bow of the vessel, in metres (overall length = A + B). 0 = not available.

B.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the stern of the vessel, in metres. 0 = not available.

C.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the port side of the vessel, in metres (beam = C + D). 0 = not available.

D.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the starboard side of the vessel, in metres. 0 = not available.

Dimension.description: Reference point for the reported position and overall vessel dimensions, given as distances in metres from the position-fixing antenna to the bow (A), stern (B), port side (C) and starboard side (D); overall length = A + B and beam = C + D.

Fixtype.description: Type of electronic position-fixing device: 0 = undefined, 1 = GPS, 2 = GLONASS, 3 = combined GPS/GLONASS, 4 = Loran-C, 5 = Chayka, 6 = integrated navigation system, 7 = surveyed, 8 = Galileo, 15 = internal GNSS.

Timestamp.description: UTC second of the minute (0-59) at which the report was generated by the originating station's electronic position-fixing system; 60 = time stamp not available (default), 61 = positioning system in manual input mode, 62 = positioning system in dead-reckoning mode, 63 = positioning system inoperative.

OffPosition.description: Off-position flag for the aid to navigation: true = the AtoN is not at its charted position (only meaningful when the time stamp is 0-59).

AtoN.description: Aid-to-Navigation status: eight status/condition bits reserved for regional or competent-authority use; 0 = default.

Raim.description: Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring (RAIM) flag of the position-fixing device: true = RAIM in use, false = RAIM not in use.

VirtualAtoN.description: Virtual aid-to-navigation flag: true = a virtual AtoN with no physical structure (synthesised by a base station), false = a real, physical AtoN.

AssignedMode.description: Assigned-mode flag: true = the station's reporting behaviour is controlled by a base station (assigned mode, via message 16/23), false = autonomous mode.

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

NameExtension.description: Continuation of the aid-to-navigation name beyond the first 20 characters (0-14 additional six-bit ASCII characters); empty when the name fits within the Name field.

schema.description: Aids-to-Navigation (AtoN) report (ITU-R M.1371 message 21) relayed by aisstream.io. It is transmitted by, or on behalf of, a physical or virtual aid to navigation to report its type, name, position, dimensions and status flags (off-position, RAIM, virtual and assigned mode).

schema.name: StaticDataReport

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this Class B static data report it is 24.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Reserved.description: Reserved field defined by ITU-R M.1371 for regional or future use; not semantically meaningful.

PartNumber.description: Selects which part of the Class B static data report this message carries: false (0) = Part A (vessel name), true (1) = Part B (ship type, vendor ID, call sign, dimensions and fix type).

ReportA.name: ReportA

Valid.description: Flag indicating Part A of the static data report was present and successfully decoded.

Name.description: Name of the vessel from Part A of the static data report, as up to 20 six-bit ASCII characters (right-padded with '@').

ReportA.description: Part A of the Class B static data report, carrying the vessel name (present when PartNumber = 0).

ReportB.name: ReportB

Valid.description: Flag indicating Part B of the static data report was present and successfully decoded.

ShipType.description: Ship and cargo type code (0-99) reported in Part B of the static data report; 0 = not available.

VendorIDName.description: Manufacturer/vendor identifier of the Class B unit (three six-bit ASCII characters of the vendor ID).

VenderIDModel.description: Vendor model code of the Class B unit (manufacturer-assigned equipment model identifier).

VenderIDSerial.description: Vendor serial number of the Class B unit (manufacturer-assigned unit serial number).

CallSign.description: International radio call sign of the vessel as up to 7 six-bit ASCII characters (right-padded with '@'); an all-'@' value means not available.

Dimension.name: Dimension

A.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the bow of the vessel, in metres (overall length = A + B). 0 = not available.

B.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the stern of the vessel, in metres. 0 = not available.

C.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the port side of the vessel, in metres (beam = C + D). 0 = not available.

D.description: Distance from the position-fixing antenna to the starboard side of the vessel, in metres. 0 = not available.

Dimension.description: Reference point for the reported position and overall vessel dimensions, given as distances in metres from the position-fixing antenna to the bow (A), stern (B), port side (C) and starboard side (D); overall length = A + B and beam = C + D.

FixType.description: Type of electronic position-fixing device: 0 = undefined, 1 = GPS, 2 = GLONASS, 3 = combined GPS/GLONASS, 4 = Loran-C, 5 = Chayka, 6 = integrated navigation system, 7 = surveyed, 8 = Galileo, 15 = internal GNSS.

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

ReportB.description: Part B of the Class B static data report, carrying ship type, vendor identification, call sign, dimensions and fix type (present when PartNumber = 1).

schema.description: Class B static data report (ITU-R M.1371 message 24) relayed by aisstream.io. The report is split into Part A (vessel name) and Part B (ship type, vendor identification, call sign, dimensions/antenna reference point and electronic fix type); PartNumber selects which part this message carries.

schema.name: BaseStationReport

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this base station report it is 4.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

UtcYear.description: UTC year of the base station's time reference (e.g. 2026); 0 = not available.

UtcMonth.description: UTC month of the base station's time reference (1-12); 0 = not available.

UtcDay.description: UTC day of month of the base station's time reference (1-31); 0 = not available.

UtcHour.description: UTC hour of the base station's time reference (0-23); 24 = not available.

UtcMinute.description: UTC minute of the base station's time reference (0-59); 60 = not available.

UtcSecond.description: UTC second of the base station's time reference (0-59); 60 = not available.

PositionAccuracy.description: Position accuracy flag: true = high accuracy (better than 10 m, e.g. DGNSS-corrected), false = low accuracy (greater than 10 m, autonomous GNSS).

Longitude.description: Longitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, east positive (range -180 to 180; 181 = position not available).

Latitude.description: Latitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, north positive (range -90 to 90; 91 = position not available).

FixType.description: Type of electronic position-fixing device: 0 = undefined, 1 = GPS, 2 = GLONASS, 3 = combined GPS/GLONASS, 4 = Loran-C, 5 = Chayka, 6 = integrated navigation system, 7 = surveyed, 8 = Galileo, 15 = internal GNSS.

LongRangeEnable.description: Flag indicating the base station is able to handle long-range AIS messages: true = capable.

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Raim.description: Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring (RAIM) flag of the position-fixing device: true = RAIM in use, false = RAIM not in use.

CommunicationState.description: SOTDMA/ITDMA communication state value carrying the synchronisation state and slot time-out / slot-allocation information used by the AIS TDMA link layer.

schema.description: AIS base station report (ITU-R M.1371 message 4) relayed by aisstream.io. A fixed shore station broadcasts it to convey the authoritative UTC date and time, its surveyed position, position-fixing device type and long-range messaging capability, allowing mobile stations to synchronise to the AIS network.

schema.name: SafetyBroadcastMessage

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this safety-related broadcast message it is 14.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Text.description: Free-text safety-related message content, encoded as six-bit ASCII characters.

schema.description: Safety-related broadcast message (ITU-R M.1371 message 14) relayed by aisstream.io. It carries free-text safety information broadcast to all stations within radio range.

schema.name: StandardSearchAndRescueAircraftReport

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this standard SAR aircraft position report it is 9.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Altitude.description: Altitude of the SAR aircraft above sea level in metres (0-4094); 4095 = not available. Derived from GNSS unless AltFromBaro indicates a barometric source.

Sog.description: Speed over ground in knots (aisstream.io reports the decoded value; AIS native resolution is 0.1 kn, 102.3 = not available).

PositionAccuracy.description: Position accuracy flag: true = high accuracy (better than 10 m, e.g. DGNSS-corrected), false = low accuracy (greater than 10 m, autonomous GNSS).

Longitude.description: Longitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, east positive (range -180 to 180; 181 = position not available).

Latitude.description: Latitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, north positive (range -90 to 90; 91 = position not available).

Cog.description: Course over ground in degrees true (0-359.9; the encoded value 3600, i.e. 360 degrees, means not available).

Timestamp.description: UTC second of the minute (0-59) at which the report was generated by the originating station's electronic position-fixing system; 60 = time stamp not available (default), 61 = positioning system in manual input mode, 62 = positioning system in dead-reckoning mode, 63 = positioning system inoperative.

AltFromBaro.description: Altitude source flag: true = altitude derived from a barometric (QNH) sensor, false = altitude derived from the GNSS receiver.

Spare1.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Dte.description: Data terminal equipment (DTE) readiness flag: false = DTE ready/available, true = DTE not available.

Spare2.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

AssignedMode.description: Assigned-mode flag: true = the station's reporting behaviour is controlled by a base station (assigned mode, via message 16/23), false = autonomous mode.

Raim.description: Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring (RAIM) flag of the position-fixing device: true = RAIM in use, false = RAIM not in use.

CommunicationStateIsItdma.description: Indicates how to interpret CommunicationState: true = the station is using ITDMA (incremental) access, false = SOTDMA (self-organising) access.

CommunicationState.description: SOTDMA/ITDMA communication state value carrying the synchronisation state and slot time-out / slot-allocation information used by the AIS TDMA link layer.

schema.description: Standard search-and-rescue (SAR) aircraft position report (ITU-R M.1371 message 9) relayed by aisstream.io. Aircraft engaged in SAR operations use it to report altitude, position, course and speed over ground for coordination with surface units.

schema.name: LongRangeAisBroadcastMessage

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this long-range AIS broadcast it is 27.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

PositionAccuracy.description: Position accuracy flag: true = high accuracy (better than 10 m, e.g. DGNSS-corrected), false = low accuracy (greater than 10 m, autonomous GNSS).

Raim.description: Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring (RAIM) flag of the position-fixing device: true = RAIM in use, false = RAIM not in use.

NavigationalStatus.description: Navigational status code (ITU-R M.1371): 0 = under way using engine, 1 = at anchor, 2 = not under command, 3 = restricted manoeuvrability, 4 = constrained by her draught, 5 = moored, 6 = aground, 7 = engaged in fishing, 8 = under way sailing, 9-13 = reserved/special categories, 14 = AIS-SART/MOB/EPIRB active, 15 = undefined (default).

Longitude.description: Longitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, east positive, at the reduced 1/10-minute resolution used by long-range reports; 181 = position not available.

Latitude.description: Latitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, north positive, at the reduced 1/10-minute resolution used by long-range reports; 91 = position not available.

Sog.description: Speed over ground in knots at the reduced resolution used by long-range reports (0-62); 63 = not available.

Cog.description: Course over ground in degrees true at the reduced resolution used by long-range reports (0-359); 511 = not available.

PositionLatency.description: Position latency flag: false = the reported position is the current GNSS position (latency less than 5 s), true = the reported position may be older than 5 s.

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

schema.description: Long-range AIS broadcast / position report for long-range applications (ITU-R M.1371 message 27) relayed by aisstream.io. Intended for reception by satellite and other long-range systems, it reports position at reduced (1/10-minute) resolution together with navigational status, speed and course over ground.

schema.name: AddressedSafetyMessage

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this addressed safety-related message it is 12.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

Sequenceinteger.description: Sequence number (0-3) used to pair this addressed message with its acknowledgement (message 7 or 13).

DestinationID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the addressed destination station this message is sent to.

Retransmission.description: Retransmission flag: true = this message is a retransmission of a previously sent message, false = first transmission.

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Text.description: Free-text safety-related message content, encoded as six-bit ASCII characters.

schema.description: Addressed safety-related message (ITU-R M.1371 message 12) relayed by aisstream.io. It delivers free-text safety information to a single addressed station (DestinationID) and supports sequencing and retransmission control.

schema.name: AddressedBinaryMessage

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this addressed binary message it is 6.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Sequenceinteger.description: Sequence number (0-3) used to pair this addressed message with its acknowledgement (message 7 or 13).

DestinationID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the addressed destination station this message is sent to.

Retransmission.description: Retransmission flag: true = this message is a retransmission of a previously sent message, false = first transmission.

ApplicationID.name: ApplicationID

Valid.description: Flag indicating the application identifier (DAC/FI) was present and successfully decoded.

DesignatedAreaCode.description: Designated Area Code (DAC) of the application identifier - the maritime jurisdiction or region authority that defines the binary application (e.g. 1 = international).

FunctionIdentifier.description: Function Identifier (FI) of the application identifier - selects the specific application message within the Designated Area Code.

ApplicationID.description: Binary application identifier (ITU-R M.1371 IAI) selecting the application that defines the binary payload; it comprises a Designated Area Code and a Function Identifier.

BinaryData.description: Application-specific binary payload defined by the application identifier, conveyed as a string of binary data.

schema.description: Addressed binary message (ITU-R M.1371 message 6) relayed by aisstream.io. It carries an application-specific binary payload, identified by an application identifier (DAC/FI), to a single addressed station with sequence and retransmission control.

schema.name: AssignedModeCommand

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this assigned mode command it is 16.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Commands.description: Map of the assignment commands carried by this message (keyed by command index, normally two entries), each assigning a destination station MMSI together with a slot offset and increment; the aisstream.io feed encodes each command as a string value.

schema.description: Assigned mode command (ITU-R M.1371 message 16) relayed by aisstream.io. A base station uses it to assign transmission slots and reporting behaviour (slot offset and increment) to one or two addressed mobile stations.

schema.name: BinaryAcknowledge

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this binary acknowledgement it is 7.

Destinations.description: Map of the acknowledged destinations (keyed by index, up to four entries), each identifying an addressed station and the sequence number of the binary message being acknowledged; the aisstream.io feed encodes each entry as a string value.

schema.description: Binary acknowledgement (ITU-R M.1371 message 7) relayed by aisstream.io. It acknowledges receipt of one or more addressed binary messages (message 6) back to their originators.

Valid.description: Flag indicating the application identifier (DAC/FI) was present and successfully decoded.

FunctionIdentifier.description: Function Identifier (FI) of the application identifier - selects the specific application message within the Designated Area Code.

schema.description: Binary broadcast message (ITU-R M.1371 message 8) relayed by aisstream.io. It broadcasts an application-specific binary payload, identified by an application identifier (DAC/FI), to all stations within radio range.

schema.name: ChannelManagement

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this channel management command it is 22.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Spare1.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

ChannelA.description: Primary AIS frequency channel number the command designates as channel A for the region or addressed stations.

ChannelB.description: Secondary AIS frequency channel number the command designates as channel B for the region or addressed stations.

TxRxMode.description: Transmit/receive mode the command sets: 0 = Tx A and Tx B, Rx A and Rx B (default); 1 = Tx A only, Rx A and Rx B; 2 = Tx B only, Rx A and Rx B; 3 = reserved.

LowPower.description: Transmitter power-level flag for the designated channels: true = low power (1 W), false = high power.

Area.name: Area

Longitude1.description: Longitude of the first (north-east) corner of the region the command applies to, in WGS-84 decimal degrees, east positive.

Latitude1.description: Latitude of the first (north-east) corner of the region the command applies to, in WGS-84 decimal degrees, north positive.

Longitude2.description: Longitude of the second (south-west) corner of the region the command applies to, in WGS-84 decimal degrees, east positive.

Latitude2.description: Latitude of the second (south-west) corner of the region the command applies to, in WGS-84 decimal degrees, north positive.

Area.description: Rectangular geographic region the channel-management command applies to, defined by two opposite corners (north-east and south-west) in WGS-84 decimal degrees.

Unicast.name: Unicast

AddressStation1.description: MMSI of the first station addressed by the channel-management command.

Spare2.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

AddressStation2.description: MMSI of the second station addressed by the channel-management command.

Spare3.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Unicast.description: Addressed (unicast) form of the channel-management command, carrying the MMSIs of the one or two stations the command is addressed to (used when IsAddressed is true).

IsAddressed.description: Addressing mode flag: true = the command is addressed to the specific stations in Unicast, false = it applies to the geographic region in Area (broadcast).

BwA.description: Bandwidth flag for channel A: false = default 25 kHz channel, true = 12.5 kHz channel.

BwB.description: Bandwidth flag for channel B: false = default 25 kHz channel, true = 12.5 kHz channel.

TransitionalZoneSize.description: Size of the transitional zone around the region, in nautical miles (transmitted value + 1 nm), within which stations may use either the old or the new channel assignment.

Spare4.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

schema.description: Channel management command (ITU-R M.1371 message 22) relayed by aisstream.io. A base station uses it to designate the AIS operating channels, bandwidths, transmit/receive mode, power level and transitional zone for a defined geographic region or for individually addressed stations.

schema.name: CoordinatedUTCInquiry

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this UTC and date inquiry it is 10.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Spare1.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

DestinationID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the addressed destination station this message is sent to.

Spare2.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

schema.description: UTC and date inquiry (ITU-R M.1371 message 10) relayed by aisstream.io. A station sends it to request a UTC and date response (message 11) from an addressed station, typically a base station.

schema.name: DataLinkManagementMessage

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this data link management message it is 20.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Data.description: Map of the TDMA slot reservations carried by this message (keyed by index, up to four entries), each describing a reserved block by slot offset, number of slots, time-out and increment; the aisstream.io feed encodes each reservation as a string value.

schema.description: Data link management message (ITU-R M.1371 message 20) relayed by aisstream.io. A base station broadcasts it to reserve TDMA slots (offset, number of slots, time-out and increment) for its own use, managing the AIS data link.

schema.name: GnssBroadcastBinaryMessage

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this GNSS/DGNSS broadcast binary message it is 17.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Spare1.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Longitude.description: Longitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, east positive (range -180 to 180; 181 = position not available).

Latitude.description: Latitude of the reported position in WGS-84 decimal degrees, north positive (range -90 to 90; 91 = position not available).

Spare2.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Data.description: Differential GNSS (DGNSS) correction payload broadcast by the reference station, conveyed as a string of binary data.

schema.description: GNSS/DGNSS broadcast binary message (ITU-R M.1371 message 17) relayed by aisstream.io. A base station broadcasts differential GNSS correction data, tagged with the reference-station position, for use by GNSS receivers in the area.

schema.name: GroupAssignmentCommand

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this group assignment command it is 23.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Spare1.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Longitude1.description: Longitude of the first (north-east) corner of the region the command applies to, in WGS-84 decimal degrees, east positive.

Latitude1.description: Latitude of the first (north-east) corner of the region the command applies to, in WGS-84 decimal degrees, north positive.

Longitude2.description: Longitude of the second (south-west) corner of the region the command applies to, in WGS-84 decimal degrees, east positive.

Latitude2.description: Latitude of the second (south-west) corner of the region the command applies to, in WGS-84 decimal degrees, north positive.

StationType.description: Station type the assignment applies to: 0 = all stations, 1 = Class A, 2 = all Class B, 3 = SAR airborne, 4 = Class B 'SO', 5 = Class B 'CS', 6 = inland waterways, 10 = base station coverage region (ITU-R M.1371).

ShipType.description: Ship and cargo type code (0-99) selecting which vessels the command applies to; 0 = all types.

Spare2.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

TxRxMode.description: Transmit/receive mode the command sets: 0 = Tx A and Tx B, Rx A and Rx B (default); 1 = Tx A only, Rx A and Rx B; 2 = Tx B only, Rx A and Rx B; 3 = reserved.

ReportingInterval.description: Assigned reporting interval code (ITU-R M.1371): selects the autonomous reporting rate the addressed stations must use (0 = as autonomously determined, 1 = 10 min, 2 = 6 min, 3 = 3 min, 4 = 1 min, 5 = 30 s, up to 9 = 2 s).

QuietTime.description: Assigned quiet time in minutes (0 = no quiet time; 1-15 = number of minutes during which the addressed stations must not transmit).

Spare3.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

schema.description: Group assignment command (ITU-R M.1371 message 23) relayed by aisstream.io. A base station uses it to control the reporting behaviour (reporting interval, quiet time and Tx/Rx mode) of a group of stations selected by region, station type and ship type.

schema.name: Interrogation

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this interrogation message it is 15.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Station1Msg1.name: Station1Msg1

Valid.description: Flag indicating this interrogation request slot was present and successfully decoded.

StationID.description: MMSI of the first interrogated station.

MessageID.description: AIS message type number requested from the interrogated station.

SlotOffset.description: Requested TDMA slot offset at which the interrogated station should transmit its reply (0 = no specific slot requested).

Station1Msg1.description: First message requested from interrogated station 1: the AIS message type requested and the TDMA slot offset at which the reply should be sent.

Station1Msg2.name: Station1Msg2

Valid.description: Flag indicating this second interrogation request for station 1 was present and successfully decoded.

Spare.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

MessageID.description: AIS message type number requested from the interrogated station (the second requested message).

SlotOffset.description: Requested TDMA slot offset for the reply to the second requested message (0 = no specific slot requested).

Station1Msg2.description: Second message requested from interrogated station 1 (the same station): an additional AIS message type requested and its reply slot offset.

Station2.name: Station2

Valid.description: Flag indicating the second interrogated station's request was present and successfully decoded.

Spare1.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

StationID.description: MMSI of the second interrogated station.

MessageID.description: AIS message type number requested from the second interrogated station.

SlotOffset.description: Requested TDMA slot offset at which the second interrogated station should transmit its reply (0 = no specific slot requested).

Spare2.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

Station2.description: Message requested from a second interrogated station: that station's MMSI, the AIS message type requested and the reply slot offset.

schema.description: Interrogation (ITU-R M.1371 message 15) relayed by aisstream.io. A station uses it to request specific message(s) from one or two addressed stations, optionally specifying the reply slot offset.

schema.name: MultiSlotBinaryMessage

MessageID.description: AIS message type number per ITU-R M.1371, carried in the first six bits of every AIS message; for this multiple-slot binary message with communication state it is 26.

RepeatIndicator.description: Repeat indicator set by AIS repeater stations to count how many times the message has been repeated: 0 = original transmission, 3 = do not repeat any more.

UserID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the transmitting station - the nine-digit identity of the vessel or station and the partition/event key for this stream.

Valid.description: aisstream.io decoder flag: true = the AIS sentence was decoded successfully and the fields in this message are reliable; false = the message could not be fully decoded.

DestinationIDValid.description: Flag indicating the message is addressed and the DestinationID field is valid (true) rather than a broadcast (false).

ApplicationIDValid.description: Flag indicating the application identifier (DAC/FI) is present and the ApplicationID field is valid.

DestinationID.description: Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the addressed destination station this message is sent to.

Spare1.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

ApplicationID.name: ApplicationID

Valid.description: Flag indicating the application identifier (DAC/FI) was present and successfully decoded.

DesignatedAreaCode.description: Designated Area Code (DAC) of the application identifier - the maritime jurisdiction or region authority that defines the binary application (e.g. 1 = international).

FunctionIdentifier.description: Function Identifier (FI) of the application identifier - selects the specific application message within the Designated Area Code.

ApplicationID.description: Binary application identifier (ITU-R M.1371 IAI) selecting the application that defines the binary payload; it comprises a Designated Area Code and a Function Identifier.

Payload.description: Application-specific binary payload, conveyed as a string of binary data.

Spare2.description: Spare bits reserved by ITU-R M.1371 for future use; transmitted as zero and carry no semantic meaning.

CommunicationStateIsItdma.description: Indicates how to interpret CommunicationState: true = the station is using ITDMA (incremental) access, false = SOTDMA (self-organising) access.

CommunicationState.description: SOTDMA/ITDMA communication state value carrying the synchronisation state and slot time-out / slot-allocation information used by the AIS TDMA link layer.

schema.description: Multiple-slot binary message with communication state (ITU-R M.1371 message 26) relayed by aisstream.io. It carries a larger application-specific binary payload (optionally addressed, with an application identifier) spanning multiple TDMA slots and includes the link communication state.

description: AISStream publishes vessel position, voyage, safety, and static AIS messages from AISStream public AIS firehose for AIS-equipped vessels received by the AISStream network. These events help consumers monitor mobility operations, passenger information, and traffic conditions without polling the upstream source directly.

documentation.description: Source documentation for AISStream, including upstream feed context and bridge deployment notes.
